seoTitle: Freeburn Island vs Freemans — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Freeburn Island and Freemans. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Freeburn Island vs Freemans: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Freeburn Island and Freemans.
heading: Freeburn Island vs Freemans — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Freeburn Island, New South Wales and Freemans, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Freemans has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Freeburn Island (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Freeburn Island or Freemans safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Freeburn Island has a flood risk score of 40/100, while Freemans scores 24/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Freeburn Island scores 61/100 for bushfire risk. Freemans scores 35/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Freeburn Island or Freemans?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Freemans has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.5% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
